title: Crohn's Disease
chief_complaint: I've had abdominal pain, diarrhea, and weight loss for 3 months. I go to the bathroom 6-8 times a day, sometimes with blood. I've lost 25 pounds. I also have painful red bumps on my shins and my right eye is red and painful.

physical_exam.Abdomen: RLQ tenderness with palpable mass. No rebound. Perianal skin tags and fistula opening.
physical_exam.Eyes: Right eye: Ciliary flush, irregular pupil. Uveitis confirmed on slit lamp.

hidden_data.colonoscopy: Skip lesions with deep serpiginous ulcers and cobblestone appearance in terminal ileum and ascending colon. Rectal sparing. Transmural inflammation on biopsy with non-caseating granulomas.
hidden_data.ct_enterography: Terminal ileal wall thickening with mesenteric fat stranding (creeping fat). Fibrofatty proliferation. No abscess or fistula.
hidden_data.stool: Negative for C. diff, negative for infections

correct_diagnosis: Moderate-to-Severe Ileocolonic Crohn's Disease with Extraintestinal Manifestations (Erythema Nodosum, Uveitis, Peripheral Arthritis)
key_learning_points: Crohn's = transmural granulomatous inflammation anywhere from mouth to anus. Skip lesions with rectal sparing. Fistula, abscess, stricture
Extraintestinal manifestations: Skin (erythema nodosum, pyoderma gangrenosum), eyes (uveitis, episcleritis), joints (peripheral arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis), liver (PSC)

Treatment: Induce remission with Prednisone 40mg taper or Budesonide (ileal/right colon). Biologics (anti-TNF: Infliximab, Adalimumab) for moderate-severe. Immunomodulators (Azathioprine, Methotrexate) for maintenance
Do NOT use NSAIDs (trigger flares). Smoking cessation crucial (smoking worsens Crohn's). Surgery for strictures, fistulas, abscesses - NOT curative (disease recurs)
